answer:
Well, I think the decisions we make can really impact not only ourselves but also the people around us. You know, when I'm faced with choices—big or small—I always try to consider how those choices might affect my friends and family. For example, when I was deciding which university to apply to, I realized that my choice wouldn't just affect me; it would influence my parents, who are so supportive and invested in my future.

I mean, choosing a school far away would mean less time with them, and that's something I had to weigh seriously. Also, I have a younger brother who looks up to me, and I want to set a good example for him. It's like, if I choose a school that aligns with my passions and career goals, it could inspire him to pursue his interests more freely, you know?

Another thing that comes to mind is when I volunteer at a local community center. Every time I interact with the kids there, I'm reminded of how my actions—whether it's how engaged I am or the activities I plan—can really shape their experiences. So, I try to be very deliberate about that.

But, honestly, it can be challenging to balance my needs with the needs of others. Sometimes I feel torn, especially when different desires clash. Still, I believe being mindful of how my choices affect others ultimately leads to better decisions, fostering positive relationships along the way. That's something I want to carry with me as I move on to this next chapter in life.
title: How do your decisions affect people around you? Do you consider this when making choices?
